steps.title: Fit the two balances in Desmos
steps.content: Use custom regression to avoid taking a 13th root by hand. The initial balance gives the point $(0,36100)$. The 13-year balance gives the point $(13,68071.93)$. Fit an exponential model with $y_1\sim a b^{x_1}$.

steps.title: Read the regression output
steps.content: Desmos returns $a\approx36100$ and $b\approx1.05$. In $y=a b^x$, $a$ is the starting value and $b$ is the yearly multiplier, so the model is $$A(t)=36100(1.05)^t.$$

steps.title: Compare with the choices
steps.content: Choice A has both the correct starting value and the fitted growth multiplier. Choice B has the right multiplier but wrong starting value. Choice C has the wrong starting value and a decay factor. Choice D starts correctly but uses $0.05$, which would make the balance almost zero after 13 years. Choice A is correct.
